events: CPI Jun 2026 released 8:30am ET: -0.4% MoM (exp -0.2%), +3.5% YoY (exp 3.8%) — cooler than expected
Core CPI Jun 2026: flat MoM (exp +0.2%), +2.6% YoY (exp 2.9%) — cooler than expected
Fed Chair Warsh House Financial Services testimony 10am ET (ongoing)
Bank earnings today: JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C — JPM record quarterly profit, broad beat
Fed Chair Warsh Senate Banking testimony Wed Jul 15 10am ET
FOMC rate decision Tue Jul 29 2pm ET (next meeting Jul 28-29)
rates: uncertain — Fed funds rate 3.50-3.75%; market pricing ~50% chance of July hike before CPI; Warsh hawkish in tone but gave no forward guidance; cool CPI likely reduces hike odds somewhat
note: Cool June CPI is a genuine positive but does not change the core valuation picture. The market is trading at a trailing P/E of ~25-26x against a historical median of ~18x (91st percentile). Fed Chair Warsh gave his first congressional testimony with a hawkish tone but no specific signal. Iran tensions persist. All three watchlist names with active theses (AAPL, MSFT, V) remain above their base fair values. Demand a full margin of safety before any buy.
